      "title": "2026 Iran war | Explained, United States, Israel, Strait of ... - Britannica",
      "content": "[Operation Epic Fury](https://www.britannica.com/event/2026-Iran-war), which began on February 28, 2026, is the [U.S.](https://www.britannica.com/place/United-States) code name for its joint military operations with [Israel](https://www.britannica.com/place/Israel) against [Iran](https://www.britannica.com/place/Iran). In response to the attacks by the [United States](https://www.britannica.com/place/United-States) and [Israel](https://www.britannica.com/place/Israel) on [Iran](https://www.britannica.com/place/Iran) on February 28, 2026, Iran launched retaliatory [missile](https://www.britannica.com/technology/missile) and [drone](https://www.britannica.com/technology/unmanned-aerial-vehicle) strikes targeting U.S. embassies, military installations, and oil infrastructure (including vessels in the [Strait of Hormuz](https://www.britannica.com/place/Strait-of-Hormuz)) throughout the [Middle East](https://www.britannica.com/place/Middle-East). **2026 Iran war**, conflict centered on [Iran](https://www.britannica.com/place/Iran) that was initiated by the [United States](https://www.britannica.com/place/United-States) and [Israel](https://www.britannica.com/place/Israel) on February 28, 2026, and has upended the [dynamics](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/dynamics) of the [Middle East](https://www.britannica.com/place/Middle-East). The conflict left enormous damage, thousands of people dead in Iran and [Lebanon](https://www.britannica.com/place/Lebanon), dozens dead in Israel and the [Gulf Arab states](https://www.britannica.com/place/Gulf-Arab-States), and millions of people displaced in the region, including more than one-sixth of the population in Lebanon after the [war](https://www.britannica.com/topic/war) prompted the resumption of the [Israel-Hezbollah war](https://www.britannica.com/event/Israel-Hezbollah-war). The conflict also saw the resumption of hostilities between Israel and Iran’s close ally [Hezbollah](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Hezbollah) in [Lebanon](https://www.britannica.com/place/Lebanon), which had been observing a fragile ceasefire since 2024 (*see* [Israel-Hezbollah war](https://www.britannica.com/event/Israel-Hezbollah-war)).",
